title: Omit permitted types declared in sealed class file
description: When all permitted types of a sealed class are declared in the same file, listing them explicitly is redundant and can reduce code clarity. Omitting these types simplifies the code and leverages Java's default behavior, making maintenance easier and reducing the risk of inconsistencies.
recommendation: Remove explicit permitted type declarations from sealed classes when all permitted types are in the same file.

checks.message: Remove explicit permitted type declarations from sealed classes when all permitted types are in the same file.
